sec1.title: 2.1 What is a Mixture?
sec1.content: This section discusses the core science topic: 2.1 What is a Mixture?. It explains the physical principles, chemical compositions, or biological structures of the subject matter. Observe the diagrams carefully and note down the SI units. For instance, the SI unit of force is the Newton ($N$), the unit of work is the Joule ($J$), and the unit of temperature is the Kelvin ($K$).

sec4.title: 2.4 Physical and Chemical Changes
sec4.content: Matter is anything that occupies space and has mass. For a long time, there were two schools of thought regarding the nature of matter. One school believed matter to be continuous like a block of wood, whereas the other thought that matter was made up of particles like sand. Let us perform an experiment: dissolve salt in water. The particles of salt get into the spaces between particles of water. This proves that matter is made of particles and they are extremely small. These particles possess kinetic energy and are in constant random motion.
